{"problem_standardized_math":"In Theorem 11.8, replace the assumption that $x_k \\to x^*$ by the assumption that the nondegenerate solution $x^*$ is a limit point of the sequence $\\{x_k\\}$. By extending the logic in the proof of that theorem, show that $x^*$ is in fact the only possible limit point of the sequence. (Hint: Show that $\\|J_{k+1}^{-1}r_{k+1}\\| \\leq \\tfrac{1}{2}\\|J_k^{-1}r_k\\|$ for all sufficiently large $k$, and hence that for any constant $\\epsilon>0$, the sequence $\\{x_k\\}$ satisfies $\\|x_k-x^*\\|\\leq \\epsilon$ for all sufficiently large $k$.)"}
